units.content: BFS marker propagation occurs on a dependency graph
A collision event is defined as two markers arriving at the same integer node
dependency index maps node IDs to chains and steps that depend on them
crack sources originate from inference steps with divergent or tension convergence
solid sources originate from the highest-level converged step in each chain
marker strength decays by a factor with each hop through the dependency graph
propagation halts when strength falls below activation threshold or hop count exceeds max_hops
collision strength is computed as the product of all activating sources' strengths
a collision is cross-level when its complexity levels span more than one level
the maximum hops reached across all activations is tracked
